chapter_title.ar: باب قَتْلِ الْعَمْدِ وَ شِبْهِ الْعَمْدِ وَ الْخَطَإِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 4 – Deliberate killing, and somewhat deliberate, and the erroneous (killing)

text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْعَمْدُ كُلُّ مَا اعْتَمَدَ شَيْئاً فَأَصَابَهُ بِحَدِيدَةٍ أَوْ بِحَجَرٍ أَوْ بِعَصاً أَوْ بِوَكْزَةٍ فَهَذَا كُلُّهُ عَمْدٌ وَ الْخَطَأُ مَنِ اعْتَمَدَ شَيْئاً فَأَصَابَ غَيْرَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from Abdullah Bin Muskan, from Al Halby who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The deliberation is all of what something is anticipated, so he hits it with an iron, or with a rock, or with a stick, or with a nudge, so all of these are deliberate; and the error is from anticipating something, so he hits something else’.
